skill.name: sql-query-builder
skill.description: Transforms natural language descriptions into SQL queries, execution plans, and optimization suggestions.
skill.summary: Natural language to SQL converter with execution plan analysis and performance tuning advice.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Two high-severity static findings were evaluated and dismissed as false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alert at line 4 triggered on Chinese text and UTF-8 symbols in the description field, which contains no cryptography whatsoever. The 'high file entropy' alert is caused by Chinese characters encoded in UTF-8, not binary or encrypted content. The skill consists solely of plain markdown documentation with no executable code, network requests, file system operations, environment variable access, or external command invocations. No malicious intent or security issues were identified.

content.user_title: Generate and Optimize SQL Queries from Natural Language
content.value_statement: Writing complex SQL queries requires deep database knowledge and is time-consuming for non-experts. This skill translates natural language descriptions into optimized SQL statements with execution plans and performance recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Convert natural language descriptions into valid SQL queries
Analyze query execution plans and identify bottlenecks
Suggest index optimizations and query rewrites
Support multiple SQL dialects and database systems
Explain complex SQL logic in plain language
limitations: Cannot execute queries directly against live databases
Requires human review for production-critical queries
Optimization suggestions depend on schema information provided by the user
May not support proprietary or niche database extensions

use_cases.title: Rapid Query Prototyping
use_cases.description: Data analysts use natural language to quickly generate SQL queries for ad-hoc reporting and exploration.

use_cases.title: Query Performance Tuning
use_cases.description: Developers receive execution plan analysis and optimization suggestions to fix slow-running database queries.

use_cases.title: SQL Learning Assistant
use_cases.description: Students and junior engineers learn SQL by seeing how natural language maps to structured query language.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Query Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a SQL query to find all customers who signed up in the last 30 days.

prompt_templates.title: Join and Filter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a query that joins the orders and customers tables to find total spending per customer in 2024.

prompt_templates.title: Execution Plan Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze why this query is slow and suggest optimizations: SELECT * FROM large_table WHERE status = 'pending' ORDER BY created_at DESC

prompt_templates.title: Complex Aggregation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a query to calculate monthly revenue by product category with running totals and percentage growth.

output_examples.input: Find the top 10 products by revenue in Q3 2024
output_examples.output: The skill responds with a SQL query using SUM and GROUP BY to calculate quarterly revenue per product, sorted in descending order. It includes a brief explanation of the aggregation logic and suggests adding an index on the order_date column for faster filtering.

output_examples.input: Why is my customer search query taking 5 seconds?
output_examples.output: The skill examines the query structure and explains that a missing index on the email column is causing a full table scan. It recommends creating a B-tree index and rewriting the query to use a covered index for better performance.

output_examples.input: Convert this to a window function query
output_examples.output: The skill rewrites the original self-join query using ROW_NUMBER() OVER() to rank records within partitions. It compares execution plans and shows that the window function approach reduces complexity from O(n squared) to O(n).

best_practices: Always review generated SQL in a non-production environment before deployment
Provide clear schema details and table relationships for more accurate queries
Test optimization suggestions against representative data volumes
anti_patterns: Do not run generated queries directly on production databases without review
Avoid vague natural language prompts that lack table or column context
Do not ignore database-specific syntax differences when copying queries across platforms

faq.question: Which database systems are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports standard SQL syntax and common dialects including PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQL Server, and SQLite.

faq.question: Can this skill execute queries against my database?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates SQL text only. It cannot connect to or execute queries against live databases.

faq.question: How accurate are the optimization suggestions?
faq.answer: Suggestions are based on general best practices. Actual performance depends on your specific schema, indexes, and data distribution.

faq.question: Do I need to provide my database schema?
faq.answer: Providing schema details improves accuracy, but the skill can generate generic queries based on common table structures you describe.

faq.question: Is generated SQL safe from injection attacks?
faq.answer: The skill generates parameterized query patterns where possible, but you should always use your database driver's parameter binding in production.

faq.question: Can it handle complex stored procedures?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE statements. Complex procedural logic may require manual refinement.
